element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Up to 2 jades follow player; provide healing and ranged attacks
Heavy emphasis throughout; described as 'good ones, not just stupid two-step switch puzzles'
Story-driven: defeating dragons to uncover father's fate
Incremental power progression via gold drops
Passive progression; jades grow via random drops

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating between action and puzzle-solving; reviewers noted the game does not give 'much room to breathe' and that continuous action sequences followed by difficult puzzles can feel plodding in late game

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic combat and straightforward puzzles in Tower of Light
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive complexity; introduction of new weapon types and environmental hazards (Ice Castle, Ancient Ruins)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Significant difficulty spike with dangerous environmental hazards (Droog Volcano, Platinum); continuous action-puzzle sequences noted as causing pacing issues; 'nasty tendency to make you replay long portions if you die'
notable_spikes: Platinum dungeon (final) combines all mechanics at peak difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Five distinct dungeons (Tower of Light, Ancient Ruins, Ice Castle, Droog Volcano, Platinum) interspersed with safe hub towns (Arcs, Toronto)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tower of Light as introduction dungeon

notable_level_patterns.description: Combat encounters immediately followed by logic puzzles without breathing room; creates tension but can feel plodding in late game
Each major dungeon introduces new environmental mechanics (ice, lava, etc.) as progression gate
Dragon-themed encounters tied to story progression; player seeks father by defeating successive dragon warriors
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Boss encounters throughout all five dungeons
